payload.text:
I want you to create a web application. The application is called Aureon Bank. Here is the short description of the application:
Aureon Bank is a premium digital neobank web platform designed to simulate a modern fintech banking ecosystem with fully dynamic dashboards, multi-role access control, enterprise-grade UI/UX, and investor-ready presentation quality. The system must behave like a real digital bank with complete interaction logic and no inactive elements.

And here is the requirements specification to execute:

⸻

1️⃣ BRAND

Application Name: Aureon Bank
Tagline: “Digital Banking, Elevated.”
Positioning: Premium Fintech Neobank
Personality: Secure, Elite, Innovative, Minimal

Visual Identity
• Primary Color: Midnight Blue (#0B1C2D)
• Secondary: Royal Gold (#C6A75E)
• Accent: Emerald (#00C48C)
• Background: Dark gradient fintech style
• Typography: Modern sans-serif
• Design Style: Minimal luxury fintech, glassmorphism cards

⸻

2️⃣ OBJECTIVES

Create a fully interactive enterprise-grade digital banking platform that:
• Supports Personal, Business, and Admin roles
• Simulates real financial system behavior
• Updates dashboards dynamically
• Enforces role-based access control
• Contains no dead buttons or placeholder links
• Looks production-ready and investor-ready

⸻

3️⃣ ROLE-BASED ACCESS SYSTEM (RBAC)

Roles:

1. Personal User
• Manage personal accounts
• Perform transfers
• Pay bills
• Apply for loans
• View transaction history
• Download statements

2. Business User
• Manage multiple sub-accounts
• Bulk payments
• High transfer limits
• Export reports
• View cash flow analytics

3. Admin (Super Admin)
• View all users
• Lock/unlock accounts
• Modify balances (Demo Mode only)
• Approve/reject loans
• Monitor transactions
• Access system analytics
• Toggle “Enterprise Demo Mode”

Mandatory:
• Each role must have a distinct dashboard.
• Navigation menu must adapt based on role.
• Unauthorized access must be blocked.
• All roles must be fully operational.

⸻

4️⃣ AUTHENTICATION SYSTEM
• Secure login
• Registration with role selection
• Password reset
• 2FA simulation
• Account lock after 3 failed login attempts
• Session timeout system

⸻

5️⃣ ENTERPRISE DASHBOARDS (DYNAMIC)

Personal Dashboard
• Animated total balance card
• Income vs Expenses chart
• Spending pie chart
• Recent transaction timeline
• Quick action buttons:
• Transfer
• Pay Bills
• Apply for Loan
• Download Statement

Business Dashboard
• Multi-account summary
• Cash flow chart
• Payroll widget
• Bulk payment tool
• Export report (PDF simulation)

Admin Dashboard
• Total system funds
• Total users
• Active vs Locked accounts
• Pending loans
• Real-time transaction feed
• Analytics charts

⸻

6️⃣ FINANCIAL SYSTEM LOGIC

Transfers
• Internal & external
• Beneficiary management
• Confirmation modal
• Real-time balance updates
• Dual-account transaction reflection
• Insufficient funds validation

Loans
• Loan calculator
• Application form
• Status tracking:
• Pending
• Approved
• Rejected
• Admin approval updates user balance dynamically

Account Locking
• Locked users cannot transfer or apply for loans
• Warning banner displayed
• Viewing history remains enabled

⸻

7️⃣ PRE-CREATED ENTERPRISE DEMO ACCOUNTS

🔐 Locked Personal Account

Username: DolmatBin
Password: DolmatBin01
Balance: $4,200,000.00
Status: Temporarily Locked
Transaction: Gold Payment
Message: “Account temporarily locked. Please visit nearest branch.”

⸻

✅ Active Personal Account

Username: SarahLee
Password: SarahLee01
Balance: $24,580.75

⸻

🏢 Business Account

Username: NovaCorp
Password: NovaCorp01
Balance: $1,280,450.00
Includes sub-accounts and bulk payment simulation.

⸻

🛠 Admin Account

Username: AdminMaster
Password: AdminMaster01
Full system control.

⸻

8️⃣ DYNAMIC UPDATE REQUIREMENTS

All dashboards must update automatically after:
• Transfers
• Payments
• Loan approvals
• Admin balance changes
• Account locking/unlocking

Requirements:
• No manual refresh required
• Charts update instantly
• Transaction history refreshes in real time
• Animated counter transitions
• Notification system (toast alerts)

⸻

9️⃣ MULTI-LANGUAGE SYSTEM
• English default
• Language dropdown in header
• Dynamic instant switching
• Scalable translation structure
• Localized date, currency, and number formatting

⸻

🔟 PREMIUM UI/UX REQUIREMENTS
• Dark fintech theme
• Glassmorphism cards
• Smooth micro-animations
• Hover effects
• Toast notifications
• Loading states
• Confirmation modals
• Fully responsive design
• Clean sidebar navigation
• Investor-grade landing page

⸻

1️⃣1️⃣ ENTERPRISE DEMO MODE

Admin toggle:
“Enterprise Demo Mode: ON/OFF”

When ON:
• Admin can modify balances
• Admin can simulate instant approvals
• All transactions are simulated
• No real payment gateway required

⸻

1️⃣2️⃣ ZERO DEAD ELEMENT POLICY
• Every button must perform a real action
• Every link must lead to an existing page
• Every form must validate inputs
• Every action must produce visible system feedback
• No placeholder components
• No inactive UI sections

⸻

🚨 FINAL MANDATORY INSTRUCTION

Do not stop until:
• All roles are fully operational
• All dashboards update dynamically
• All demo accounts function correctly
• All system actions reflect in real time
• All buttons, forms, dropdowns, charts, and navigation links are clickable and working
• The application behaves like a real premium fintech product

**Designing Aureon Bank's Interface**

I'm focused on the visual identity for "Aureon Bank." Initial concepts revolve around a dark gradient with glassmorphism to convey minimal luxury. The color palette will incorporate Midnight Blue, Royal Gold, and Emerald. Design will be fully responsive across all devices to provide a superior user experience.


**Defining User Roles & Access**

I've been defining the distinct functionalities for Personal, Business, and Admin user roles. Each role will have specific widgets, charts, and data access. I'm focusing on features such as secure login, multi-account management, loan applications, and real-time updates. I've also implemented account locking and 2FA simulation for security. I'm building with React 18+, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS.

diffs.replacement:
import { UserRole, UserStatus, User } from './types';

export const INITIAL_USERS: User[] = [
  {
    id: '1',
    username: 'DolmatBin',
    name: 'Dolmat Bin',
    role: UserRole.PERSONAL,
    status: UserStatus.LOCKED,
    balance: 4200000.00,
    failedLoginAttempts: 0,
    transactions: [
      { id: 't1', type: 'DEBIT', amount: 50000, category: 'Investment', description: 'Gold Payment', date: '2024-05-10' }
    ],
    loans: []
  },
  {
    id: '2',
    username: 'SarahLee',
    name: 'Sarah Lee',
    role: UserRole.PERSONAL,
    status: UserStatus.ACTIVE,
    balance: 24580.75,
    failedLoginAttempts: 0,
    transactions: [
      { id: 't2', type: 'CREDIT', amount: 5000, category: 'Salary', description: 'Monthly Payroll', date: '2024-05-01' },
      { id: 't3', type: 'DEBIT', amount: 120, category: 'Dining', description: 'Starbucks Coffee', date: '2024-05-05' }
    ],
    loans: []
  },
  {
    id: '3',
    username: 'NovaCorp',
    name: 'Nova Corporation',
    role: UserRole.BUSINESS,
    status: UserStatus.ACTIVE,
    balance: 1280450.00,
    failedLoginAttempts: 0,
    subAccounts: [
      { id: 's1', name: 'Main Operating', balance: 1000000.00, type: 'CHECKING' },
      { id: 's2', name: 'Payroll Reserve', balance: 250000.00, type: 'PAYROLL' },
      { id: 's3', name: 'Tax Savings', balance: 30450.00, type: 'SAVINGS' }
    ],
    transactions: [],
    loans: []
  },
  {
    id: '4',
    username: 'AdminMaster',
    name: 'Super Admin',
    role: UserRole.ADMIN,
    status: UserStatus.ACTIVE,
    balance: 0,
    failedLoginAttempts: 0,
    transactions: [],
    loans: []
  }
];

export const TRANSLATIONS = {
  EN: {
    welcome: 'Welcome back',
    balance: 'Total Balance',
    transfer: 'Transfer',
    payBills: 'Pay Bills',
    applyLoan: 'Apply for Loan',
    history: 'Transaction History',
    analytics: 'Analytics',
    logout: 'Logout',
    accounts: 'Accounts',
    settings: 'Settings',
    demoMode: 'Enterprise Demo Mode',
    status: 'Status',
    role: 'Role',
    action: 'Actions'
  },
  ES: {
    welcome: 'Bienvenido de nuevo',
    balance: 'Saldo Total',
    transfer: 'Transferir',
    payBills: 'Pagar Facturas',
    applyLoan: 'Solicitar Préstamo',
    history: 'Historial',
    analytics: 'Analítica',
    logout: 'Cerrar Sesión',
    accounts: 'Cuentas',
    settings: 'Ajustes',
    demoMode: 'Modo Demo',
    status: 'Estado',
    role: 'Rol',
    action: 'Acciones'
  },
  FR: {
    welcome: 'Bon retour',
    balance: 'Solde Total',
    transfer: 'Transfert',
    payBills: 'Payer Factures',
    applyLoan: 'Demande de prêt',
    history: 'Historique',
    analytics: 'Analytique',
    logout: 'Déconnexion',
    accounts: 'Comptes',
    settings: 'Paramètres',
    demoMode: 'Mode Démo',
    status: 'Statut',
    role: 'Rôle',
    action: 'Actions'
  }
};

entries.description: Centralized state management for the bank

import React from 'react'; import { useBank } from '../../context/BankContext'; import { useToast } from '../Toast'; import { CreditCard, ArrowUpRight, ArrowDownLeft, FileText, Send, PieChart as PieChartIcon, TrendingUp } from 'lucide-react'; import { FinancialGrowthChart, SpendingPieChart } from '../Charts'; import { UserStatus } from '../../types'; export const PersonalDashboard: React.FC<{ onTransferClick: () => void; onLoanClick: () => void; }> = ({ onTransferClick, onLoanClick }) => { const { state } = useBank(); const { showToast, ToastContainer } = useToast(); const user = state.currentUser!; const growthData = [ { name: 'Jan', value: 20000 }, { name: 'Feb', value: 22000 }, { name: 'Mar', value: 21500 }, { name: 'Apr', value: 24000 }, { name: 'May', value: user.balance }, ]; const pieData = [ { name: 'Investments', value: 40 }, { name: 'Dining', value: 15 }, { name: 'Shopping', value: 25 }, { name: 'Utilities', value: 20 }, ]; const handleDownloadStatement = () => { showToast('Generating secure PDF statement...', 'info'); setTimeout(() => showToast('Statement downloaded successfully', 'success'), 2000); }; return (
{ToastContainer} {user.status === UserStatus.LOCKED && (

Security Alert

Account temporarily locked. Please visit the nearest branch or contact premium support.

)} {/* Hero Stats */}

Total Liquid Assets

${user.balance.toLocaleString(undefined, { minimumFractionDigits: 2 })}

Quick Actions

{/* Analytics */}

Portfolio Growth

Spending Analysis

{/* Transactions */}

Recent Ledger Activity

{user.transactions.length > 0 ? user.transactions.map(t => ( )) : ( )}
Transaction Category Date Amount
{t.description} {t.category} {t.date} {t.type === 'CREDIT' ? '+' : '-'}${t.amount.toLocaleString()}
No recent activity detected
); };

import React from 'react'; import { useBank } from '../../context/BankContext'; import { useToast } from '../Toast'; import { LayoutGrid, Users, BarChart3, Download, Plus, Zap } from 'lucide-react'; import { CashFlowChart } from '../Charts'; export const BusinessDashboard: React.FC = () => { const { state } = useBank(); const { showToast, ToastContainer } = useToast(); const user = state.currentUser!; const cashFlowData = [ { name: 'Mon', income: 45000, expense: 32000 }, { name: 'Tue', income: 52000, expense: 38000 }, { name: 'Wed', income: 48000, expense: 42000 }, { name: 'Thu', income: 61000, expense: 35000 }, { name: 'Fri', income: 55000, expense: 44000 }, ]; const handleBulkPayment = () => { showToast('Initializing Bulk Payment Module...', 'info'); setTimeout(() => { showToast('24 Corporate payouts processed successfully.', 'success'); }, 2000); }; return (
{ToastContainer}

Total Corporate Assets

${user.balance.toLocaleString()}

{user.subAccounts?.map(sub => (
{sub.type}

{sub.name}

${sub.balance.toLocaleString()}

))}

Cash Flow

Revenue (MTD) +$240,500
OpEx (MTD) -$128,400

Global Cash Movement

Team Payroll

Next Payroll

June 01, 2024

$12,450.00

Departments

Engineering $45,000
Marketing $22,000
Management $30,000
); };

entries.description: Main layout with sidebar and header

payload.text: every write Demo should be replaced with ACCOUNTS

**Analyzing Replacement Scenarios**

I'm currently focused on the implications of replacing "Demo" with "ACCOUNTS." I've begun by analyzing `types.ts`, specifically the `enterpriseDemoMode` variable and wondering whether I should leave it as-is, since this update relates to UI labels. I'm carefully considering how to scope the replacement to UI elements to avoid unintended consequences in code logic.


**Refining Scope of Replacement**

I've moved on to examining the specific UI strings and identifying the appropriate replacements. I'm focusing on the `constants.ts` file first, updating the "Enterprise Demo Mode" string to "Enterprise ACCOUNTS Mode." I'm also considering the implications of the uppercase "ACCOUNTS" used by the user, and if this relates to every instance of the usage of the word "demo." I'm also ensuring the replacement considers various file paths.


**Clarifying "Every Write"**

I'm now honing in on the user's intent with "every write." It seems they mean any instance of "Demo" in the written UI, not the code logic, which simplifies the task. I'll update labels in `constants.ts`, `App.tsx` (login buttons), and `AdminDashboard.tsx`. I'll keep the `enterpriseDemoMode` state property in `BankContext.tsx` and `types.ts`, as the prompt focused on written replacements.


**Implementing UI String Updates**

I'm now implementing the UI string updates based on the examples provided. I've updated the strings in `constants.ts`, `AdminDashboard.tsx`, and `App.tsx` as requested. I've also double-checked `context/BankContext.tsx`, just in case there were unexpected instances of the word. I also looked for "Demo" in other places, in case the instructions were unclear.


**Updating Variable Names**

I'm now focusing on variable names, as I re-evaluated the user's "every write" instruction. Given this literal interpretation, I'll update `enterpriseDemoMode` to `enterpriseACCOUNTSMode` in `types.ts` and `context/BankContext.tsx`. I'll make the requested changes in `constants.ts`, `App.tsx`, and `AdminDashboard.tsx`, as previously planned.


**Implementing Comprehensive Updates**

I'm now methodically applying the replacements across all specified files. I've updated `types.ts` by renaming `TOGGLE_DEMO_MODE` and `enterpriseDemoMode` to `TOGGLE_ACCOUNTS_MODE` and `enterpriseACCOUNTSMode` respectively. In `constants.ts`, I'm carefully updating translations to accurately reflect the "ACCOUNTS" replacement. Finally, I'm working through the state property and action type in `context/BankContext.tsx`, making the necessary adjustments for consistency. I am ensuring that I am adhering to the precise case instructions.


**Finalizing Replacement Details**

I'm now implementing all the final replacements. Specifically, in `AdminDashboard.tsx` and `App.tsx`, I am methodically updating labels and logic, which includes login buttons, and toast notifications. I have also replaced `enterpriseDemoMode` to `enterpriseACCOUNTSMode` in `types.ts`, and in `BankContext.tsx`, and I've also replaced `TOGGLE_DEMO_MODE` to `TOGGLE_ACCOUNTS_MODE`, as per the "every write" instruction, and also as requested by the user. Finally, I've checked to ensure all UI elements are updated.

entries.description: Rename Demo references to ACCOUNTS in types
Rename Demo references to ACCOUNTS in constants and translations
